column2: On uneven paths the heel feels wobbly side‑to‑side. Slanted pavements bring a quick aching shift under the ankle when I step.

column3: The feverish feeling eased, but I’m now breathing faster with a dry, exhausting cough. I get light‑headed after climbing stairs and feel like I can’t fill my lungs completely.

column2: A short sprint brought on a twinge in the middle of the back of my thigh. It’s tender along a band and stiffens if I sit too long.

column3: I accidentally left my hand on a hot mug for a minute. It now feels very tender, and there's a small blister.

column2: A sudden twist while reaching for a bag made one side of my lower back feel ‘switched off’ and sore. Small stabilizing movements feel shaky.

column3: During a birthday party, a hot beverage was knocked onto my back. The area is red, tender, and a small blister is visible on my shoulder.

column2: I noticed a rash on my forearms after handling a new brand of sunscreen. The spots are itchy and raised. I didn’t realize it could be an allergic reaction until it started spreading. I’ve stopped using the product.

column3: Since I lost 20 pounds, my headaches have lessened, but I still feel lightheaded when I stand up. I believe my diet is the culprit. I haven't checked my blood pressure.

column2: I feel an unexplained pressure behind my eyes and a dull ache in my neck. It’s mild but persistent.

column3: I slipped on ice and landed seated. Since then sitting on hard chairs is very uncomfortable, and leaning slightly forward helps. Standing up from sitting is a careful process.

column2: There’s a small sore area on the top of my foot that flares when I push off while walking. Tight shoes make it throb and I limp by the evening.
I have an increased thirst level and I’m going to the bathroom more than usual. I’ve been losing weight without effort. The doctor suspected a blood sugar disorder.

column3: On the sole, a painful spot feels like stepping on a stone. The surface is rough with tiny dark dots

column2: Where my collarbone meets the chest feels puffy by evening and aches when I push up from the floor. Reaching across my body brings a small jab there.

column3: I woke up with a runny nose and a sore throat, but I thought it was just a bad cold. The congestion hasn't cleared even after I tried decongestants. I have been feeling slightly feverish and tired.

After eating lunch, I noticed a rash on my arms. The spots were itchy and seemed to spread over my chest. I haven't changed my diet or taken any new medications. I’m concerned.

column2: My right ear hurts like a stone in the middle. The pain is stronger when I drive. I had a flu 5 days ago and it started soon after.

column3: My right ear hurts when I swallow, and I feel pressure when I sit in the car. It’s been a few days since my flu. I have a slight fever and a sore throat.
I noticed my hands have started to feel numb and tingly, especially after standing for a while. I also keep having headaches, and my vision feels fuzzy when I drive.

column2: By late afternoon my eyelids feel heavy and my smile looks uneven in photos. Climbing stairs is fine in the morning but by evening my legs tire quickly and I need the rail.

column3: I felt better for a day after the flu‑like symptoms, then the cough came back heavier with chills. There’s a sharp ache on the right side when I take a deep breath and I’m more wiped out again.

column2: I have a nagging headache and occasional chest tightness that appears in the late afternoon. It’s not painful but it is persistent.

I can’t seem to get enough air after a run and I have this persistent cough that doesn’t clear up. I still think it’s just over‑exertion.

column3: I drift sideways when I try to walk a straight line, and quick turns make me wobble. Reaching for a glass, my hand overshoots a bit before I correct it.

column2: I noticed a strange numb patch in the saddle area and it’s harder to feel when I wipe. My legs feel heavy after a short walk and I move more cautiously.

My doctor says my lungs feel tight when I sneeze; it's like I can't exhale properly.

column3: I woke up with a minor headache, congestion, and a dry cough. I stayed home and took over-the-counter pain relievers. By the end of the day, I felt normal.

column2: I keep waking up at night because my ear hurts so badly. I also feel like something is blocking my ear.

column3: My nose was runny and my throat felt scratchy, but I didn't think it was anything serious. A friend mentioned a 'cold', and I realized it was that.

column2: I've been feeling unusually fatigued and can't concentrate at my desk. My eyes feel strained and I sometimes hear a buzzing in my ears. My wife has noticed I get short of breath when walking up stairs.

column3: I forgot my hand inside a boiling pot while making soup. The left forearm is now bright red, itching, and a small blister is forming.
My ears felt full and my nose was runny, but I didn't consider it a cold. A colleague told me it was typical of a common cold.
I’ve been increasing my runs and now have a pinpoint ache on the inside of my shin. It’s tender to touch and hurts even during easy jogs, worse on hard pavement.
My sister told me I cough a lot at night, and it seems like I can't catch my breath in the middle of the night.
Between my toes the skin is itchy, cracked, and peels. After workouts the area stings and smells stronger

column2: I was painting a fence and accidentally sprayed a can of paint on my arm. The area looks like a sunburn.

column3: Over a couple of days, vision in one eye dimmed like a grey filter. Moving the eye hurts slightly, and bright colors look washed out compared to the other eye.
My doctor said my hemoglobin is low, but I didn't realize it would affect me that much. I get winded when I walk up stairs.
I woke up with a rash of hives on my forearms and upper legs. They feel like small, itchy bumps. The itching is severe and I’ve been scratching all night.
The doctor told me my blood pressure is high, but I didn't realize until they mentioned it. I never had any symptoms. I have been on medication for high cholesterol.

column2: I keep getting headaches and feeling exhausted at the office. My face looks washed out and I feel sluggish all day.

column3: Breathing has become very labored over the day; even speaking full sentences is difficult. I feel like I can’t get enough air, and I need to sit very still to avoid getting winded.

column2: I rolled my ankle stepping off a curb. There’s a tender point along the outside bone above the ankle and it’s hard to put weight without a sharp twinge.

column3: After a long hike, lifting my knee and turning the hip outward gives a pulling pain from the outer hip to the inner knee. Sitting cross‑legged is uncomfortable.

column2: I left a mug too hot on my desk and accidentally touched it. The right wrist feels like it’s on fire and has a blister.

column3: A sudden rash has covered my chest and upper arms. They are red, itchy, and seem to have a fine texture. The hives started after an allergy test.

column2: Reaching overhead repeatedly left a dull ache at the top-back of the shoulder. Lifting the arm to the side feels weak and I avoid heavy objects on that side.

column3: Weeks after a winter bug, I still cough at night and when I laugh. I keep a glass of water by the bed because talking sets it off.

column2: Turning a stiff key gives a line of soreness along the inner front of my forearm. Pouring from a heavy pan with the palm down brings a quick sting.

column3: I woke up with itchy bumps all over my arms and chest, and they keep popping up whenever I touch my skin. It feels like a fine sandpaper under my clothes. I haven't noticed any pattern or trigger. I thought it might just be a bad rash.

column2: I feel like my ear is clogged and I can hear faint noises. I have a slight fever and feel tired.

column3: On my chest there are uneven pale and slightly darker spots that don’t tan like the rest. When I rub the area, a fine scale appears on the surface.

column2: I have headaches that come and go throughout the day and a slight burning sensation in my neck. I also notice that I feel a bit tired even after a night's sleep.

column3: I left a cup of hot cocoa on my desk and touched it. The skin on my hand is red, slightly swollen, and a tiny blister is developing.
My mouth is always dry, and I get infections in my gums. I wake up often at night to pee.

column2: I noticed a sudden feeling of nausea and headache after taking a cold medicine. I thought it was a side effect. My father had high blood pressure.

column3: I start feeling a dull pressure in my head before I’m able to sleep. The pain flares up when I eat something sweet, especially chocolate.

column2: My child has been crying a lot because his ear hurts. I heard a faint ringing sound from his ear.

column3: I felt a slight fever and congestion that morning, but I brushed it off as a mild cold. A quick nap helped, and I returned to work the next morning.

column2: There’s a persistent itchy-burny patch just inside my shoulder blade that I can’t quite reach. The skin looks normal but the urge to scratch comes and goes.

column3: I had a rash on my neck after getting a haircut. The spots were itchy, and they appeared across my neck and shoulder. I haven’t noticed any hair products causing it. I’m confused.

column2: I feel a lot of fatigue and my vision blurs after meals. I also notice numbness in my hands. The doctor recommended a blood test.

column2: In the mornings I cough more and bring up thicker phlegm than I used to. A short trip to the shop leaves me winded and I need a quick rest at the door.

column3: My head keeps wanting to turn slightly to one side, and the neck muscles feel tight like they’re pulling. Stress makes it more noticeable, especially when I’m standing in line.

column3: On my chest there are uneven pale and slightly darker spots that don't tan like the rest. When I rub the area, a fine scale appears on the surface.
After a long gym session, the outer front of my forearm feels numb and tingles when I touch the skin. Lifting light weights is fine, but the skin sensation is off.
After push‑ups, the back of my upper arm feels tight and sore, especially when I try to straighten the elbow fully. Leaning on the arm increases the ache.
The back of the neck and scalp are itchy, especially behind the ears. I can spot small, firm white specks stuck to hair near the roots that don’t brush off.

column2: The room spins when I turn my head and I need to hold onto walls to walk straight. Looking down while moving makes me nauseous.
Squatting to pick something up gave a sharp pinch inside the knee. Since then it clicks and occasionally feels stuck for a second when I bend.

column3: I feel light‑headed, especially when standing. I also notice my face looks washed out.
On a camping trip, I set up a grill outdoors. The heat from the grill touched my shoulder, causing redness.
After a high step down, my heel took the brunt and now it’s very painful to put weight on it. The sides of the heel feel sore when I squeeze them.

column2: I get headaches and feel light‑headed when I get up. My face looks washed out and I keep feeling tired.

column3: I spilled a cup of hot coffee on my forearm and it’s now a bright red, painful spot. I see a few tiny blisters forming and the skin feels hot to touch.
Using my phone one‑handed all day made the base of my thumb feel tired and sore. Pinching to open a packet gives a short, sharp twinge.

column2: Rolling my ankle outward left a sore spot just behind the outer ankle bone. Side steps or uneven ground make it twinge.

column3: I’m noticing a wheeze and shortness of breath after walking up a flight of stairs, even though I haven’t been sick recently.

I've been feeling unusually tired lately, even after a good night's sleep. My skin looks paler than usual, and I get winded walking up the stairs. I also feel dizzy when I stand up quickly. I haven't seen a doctor yet because I think it's just stress.

column2: I keep coughing and feel like my chest is tight whenever I get to the top of stairs. I never considered that could be an asthma trigger.

column3: Over the last two weeks, a persistent itch has spread to the waistline and wrists. I notice little burrow‑like marks and tiny bumps that keep me awake.
A new brown patch on my back looks waxy and slightly raised, almost like it’s stuck onto the skin. It doesn’t hurt but sometimes catches on clothing.

column2: I had a light cough and congestion that evening after a day at the office. I realized it was a cold when I started to feel a mild headache.
During my shower my ear started hurting and I couldn't hear properly. I have a slight headache.

column3: Lifting a suitcase gave me a grab at one side of the lower back. Bending to the opposite side aches, and standing still too long makes it tighten.

column2: I notice a dull headache that starts in the morning and stays all day. I assumed it was a migraine. My blood pressure reading was higher than normal.
Sleeping awkwardly gave me a sore band from the neck to the top of the shoulder. Looking over my shoulder or holding the phone there tightens it.

column3: I’ve lost some weight without trying, and I keep feeling tired. My thirst level has gone up a lot, especially after meals.

column2: Shrugging one shoulder feels weaker and the shoulder sits lower by the end of the day. Holding a bag on that side makes my neck tire fast.
Twisting my ankle weeks ago left a lingering deep ache inside the joint. It clicks occasionally and aches after longer walks.
I experience a sudden rush of blood pressure after taking an OTC painkiller. I assumed it was the medication side effect. I haven't had a check-up in 3 years.
My child's first birthday party had a cake with hot butter; he touched the pan. His hand looks red and swollen.
I feel exhausted during my day job and my breath catches during simple tasks. I also notice my skin is pale. I get headaches after long meetings. I assume it's a stress reaction.

column3: Tight shoes make the space between my first and second toes burn and tingle. Lacing the shoes loosely helps for a while, then the buzz returns on walks.

column2: I spilled hot tea on my shirt; the fabric touched my chest. Now I have a rash and slight pain.

column3: I was told my blood pressure is elevated during a routine visit, but I had no symptoms. I didn't understand the significance. I was taking a blood pressure monitor at home.
My child got a small scrape near the mouth, and then honey‑colored crusts formed around it. It spreads easily at school so we’re cleaning the area often and keeping hands away from the face.

column2: There’s a persistent burning feeling on the tongue and palate even though the mouth looks normal. Hot drinks make it flare and it eases slightly with cold water.
I get a strange sensation in my chest when I walk quickly and it feels like I'm being pressed.

column3: I woke up with a stuffy nose and a scratchy throat that feels like a sandpaper. I keep sneezing and my throat hurts when I swallow. By evening, I'm feeling a bit tired but still able to finish my work.
I've noticed that my breathing gets worse in cold air, and I often feel short‑of‑breath when I open my windows in winter.
I've been experiencing a persistent cough after eating spicy food, and it's been getting worse over the past few weeks.

column2: I woke up with a dull ache in both ears. The pain is more intense when I swallow. I had a sore throat and mild fever for the last two days.

column3: I’ve been coughing in the evenings for a while now, and after I go for a short walk I feel a tightness in my chest. It’s getting more frequent.

column2: I've been experiencing a constant pressure behind my eyes and a feeling of being drained after short walks. My wife reports that I have difficulty sleeping because of a vague tightness in my chest.

column3: My headache started last week, it’s a pressure behind my eyes and I feel nauseated. It flares up after I finish a long workday and I can’t eat because I feel queasy.
I feel a throbbing sensation in my temples after a long flight. It worsens if I’m not moving, and my vision flickers.
I've been having this throbbing pain that starts at the back of my head and moves forward. My vision gets worse and my ears ring. I've tried sleeping, but the pain starts again when I wake.

column2: I experience headaches and feel lightheaded. My chest feels tight during daily tasks. My skin looks pale and my eyes look bright. I didn't think much of it until my mom noticed.

column3: Whenever I breathe in cold air, I get this wheeze and feel my lungs constrict. I thought it was just a reaction to the cold.
After adding hill repeats, I feel a long, dull ache along the inner shin during and after runs. Pressing the area is sore along a stretch rather than one spot.
I was fixing a car and the metal engine was hot. I touched it with my hand, and now there's a burning feeling.

column2: I noticed a dry cough and a little headache after a movie night. I took some rest and stayed away from the office for the rest of the day.
I was using a stove without a pot; my fingers burned. There’s a small blister and the skin is sore when I flex my fingers.

My 7‑year‑old has had very itchy patches in the elbow creases and behind the knees for about a week. The skin is very dry and sometimes peels; nighttime itching wakes him up. It flared after we changed laundry detergent. We’re moisturizing morning and evening and trying to limit scratching.

column3: After brisk walking downhill, the front of my shin aches and lifting my foot while stepping is uncomfortable. It stiffens again after sitting.
After I finished a brisk walk, my chest felt tight and I started coughing, which I hadn’t experienced before. I’m unsure if this is normal.
After my brother's allergies flare up, I noticed I also start wheezing when I run, especially in the evening. I keep thinking it's just me being out of shape.
Yesterday I felt a sudden, sharp pain in my ear that made me cry. I thought it was just a cold, but the pain didn't go away.
Around the nose, tiny blisters burst and left sticky yellowish crusts. We’re keeping the skin clean and reminding not to scratch.

column2: My wife told me I wheeze when I laugh too hard; it's like my chest is under pressure.

column3: By late afternoon there’s a tight band around my head, like a clamp at my temples. It eases a bit with a short walk, but screen time brings it back.
During a meeting, the lights became too bright and I felt my head start to hurt. I experienced a strange flashing light before the pain, and then my vision blurred.
I developed a rash on my upper chest after being on a bus. The spots were itchy, raised, and spread quickly. I haven’t had any new medications or foods. I’m unsure what’s causing it.

column2: Tighter shoes rub the big‑toe joint and it swells by evening. Long walks make the area sore and I prefer wider footwear.

column3: I keep feeling fatigued even after sleeping. I notice my chest feels tight during light exertion. My eyes look bright, as if something is wrong. I've been treating it as a bad flu.
Over a few weeks, my teen’s limp has worsened and hip or knee aches after PE class. Turning the leg inward seems limited and he takes shorter steps.
I get a dull ache that becomes a sharp, throbbing pain. It comes with nausea and my skin feels hot. I've tried resting, but it doesn't cure it.
After lifting a box awkwardly, there’s a tender spot where the collarbone meets the chest. Pushing up from the floor gives a sharp jab right there.
After a long walk, the inside of my ankle aches and pushing off the big‑toe side of the foot is uncomfortable. Hilly streets aggravate it.

Since last week, random spots have appeared on my back and legs. They flare up when I walk or run, and the itching makes me scratch them, which only makes them worse. No allergic foods or medications seem related. It’s getting harder to stay in the office.
Suddenly I feel tight at the throat when I breathe in during a fast walk, with a noisy rasp for a few seconds. Slowing down and focusing on calm breaths settles it.

column2: My ear hurts and I hear less of my friends speaking. I also feel a bit nauseated.

column3: After a recent flu, I felt a persistent pressure in my chest and a dull ache in my back. The discomfort came on slowly and was not associated with any specific activity.
Coming down hard on my heel from a step, my ankle took a heavy jolt. Now the front of the ankle is very sore, swollen, and I can barely put weight on it.
Lifting a box, I felt a grab in my lower back. Standing up straight is uncomfortable and bending forward to tie shoes brings a guarded, tight sensation.
I’ve been feeling unusually fatigued all day, even after a good night's rest. Occasionally my vision blurs when I stare at my computer for too long.
After a flu, I noticed my breathing became more labored and I have a faint whistling sound when I exhale. I just thought it was the flu.
I spent a few hours outside without a hat, and my scalp feels like it's on fire. The redness is spreading.

column2: I have been experiencing shortness of breath during my daily walks. My hands feel cold and my eyes look tired even when I'm well‑rested.
I get a pulse‑like pain that starts on my left side and spreads. The pain is so strong that I can't keep my eyes open. It feels like something in my brain is vibrating.

column3: After long uphill walks, there’s a small, sharp spot deep in the buttock when I take a long step. Shorter steps ease it.

column2: I have a rash on my upper back and shoulders. The welts feel like a raised grid and are very itchy. It started after a cold last week.
I felt tingling at the corner of my lip and then grouped blisters appeared. The area is sore and tight when I smile or eat. I'm dabbing it gently and keeping the skin clean.

column3: I noticed a mild headache and congestion the evening before my work meeting. I decided to stay home and rest, and the next day I felt like myself again.

column2: I woke up with a sharp pain in my left ear and slight pressure. I have a mild fever and a sore throat. My hearing feels muffled.

column3: I developed a rash on my chest after being in a hot room for a while. The spots were raised, itchy, and seemed to spread. I’m not sure if I should stay cool or avoid the heat entirely.

column2: Twisting a jar lid hurts on the little‑finger side of my wrist. Leaning on that hand or bearing weight through it brings a sharp jab.
I get pain in my ear when I lift my chin. I also feel a slight pressure and a muffled sound.
A sudden peppery tickle appears for no reason and I cough in short bursts. The throat feels normal between episodes and voice teas help only briefly.

column3: Over a few weeks my shoulder has become stiff in all directions. Reaching for the back pocket or putting on a coat is tight and achy, especially mornings.
Bending backward to reach a high shelf pinches in my lower back. Standing still too long makes it stiff, and it eases a bit when I walk.

column2: The big‑toe joint feels blocked when I try to bend it up during a brisk walk. Certain shoes make it throb by the evening.

column3: Cold air and climbing one flight of stairs give me a tight, whistly feeling in my chest. If I slow down, it eases after a minute, but brisk walks bring it back.
I’ve noticed that I get dizzy when I stand up quickly, and sometimes I feel like my ears are ringing. The dizziness has become more frequent, especially after I finish a long meeting at work.

column2: I’ve had a cut on my hand that won’t heal. It’s also been hard to concentrate and I feel foggy. I was surprised to learn my doctor thinks this might be linked to my blood glucose levels.

column3: Side steps and crossing my legs feel unsteady, with a vague numbness in the inner thigh. Squeezing the knees together feels weaker than usual.

column2: I experience a pressure in my shoulders after a stressful meeting. I thought it was a tense muscle. I have been drinking more coffee lately.

column3: My headache begins after a meeting with loud music and I feel a sudden pressure behind my eyes. The pain spreads across my face, and my stomach feels upset.
One afternoon I repeatedly asked what day it was and couldn’t remember a short walk I had just taken. Hours later it cleared, but that period is blank.

column2: I was in the sun without a shirt during a picnic; my back got a bright red patch. The skin feels tight.

column3: Every time I go into the gym after a holiday, I start coughing and find myself short of breath, but I still attribute it to the heavy air conditioning.
After a coughing spell, bracing my belly to roll out of bed gives a thin band of soreness across the lower abdomen. Laughing brings it back.

column2: I noticed a dull pain in my left ear while listening to music. The ear feels full and I can’t hear well. It’s been two days since my upper respiratory infection.
I landed awkwardly and the front of the ankle feels jammed and very tender. Pointing the toes down or up is painful and walking is stiff.

column3: A round, expanding patch with a scaly edge developed on my forearm. The center looks clearer while the border is slightly raised and itchy.
My appetite is normal, but I keep losing weight. I also feel extremely thirsty all the time.
After hill sprints, pushing up from a squat gives a deep ache in the buttock. Climbing stairs feels weak on that side and I avoid low chairs.
After a bad misstep, I felt a deep, persistent thigh pain that makes weight‑bearing uneasy. The leg aches even at rest and I guard each movement.

column2: My finger twisted when a dog leash yanked; it’s swollen and sore along the whole length. Making a fist is painful and the finger drifts when I try.

column3: There’s a nagging ache deep in my thigh that’s worse at night. Oddly, after taking an over‑the‑counter pain tablet, it eases for a few hours and then creeps back.
Woke up with a stiff neck after an awkward sleep. Turning to check my blind spot is tight and painful on one side, and looking up makes it pull.
After reaching overhead to paint for a few hours, I’ve got a deep ache along the back of my ribs that tugs when I reach forward or lift a box from the floor.
My shoulder feels stiff and achy reaching up to a cupboard. First thing in the morning it’s the worst, then it loosens a bit after moving around.

column2: I was on a flight and after landing my ear felt very painful and I couldn't hear properly.

column3: After a family cold, my chest feels rattly and I get winded on small hills. Hot showers make the cough worse in the morning.
My pain starts on my right side after a long drive, and it’s got worse since then. I also feel nauseated and the light in the car makes it worse.

column2: Pushing a heavy door gave me a tug under the shoulder blade along the ribs. Taking a deep breath stretches it in a sharp line.
I get sudden itchy spots on my back after cleaning the house. The welts appear and disappear after a few hours. I’ve not used any new cleaning products. It’s starting to bother me.

column3: I noticed that when I stand near the stove, my breathing slows and I begin coughing.
I keep coughing at night, but I thought it was just a cold. The next morning, my chest feels tight and I find myself gasping for a breath after a short walk.

I've noticed a clear runny nose and a mild headache, but I'm not feeling feverish at all. My throat has a mild soreness and I cough sometimes when I clear my throat. It started a day ago and I expect it to clear up soon.
After a forceful throw, the inner elbow felt sore and opening a door with the palm forward gives a sharp twinge. Leaning on that elbow is uncomfortable.

column2: I get a sharp pain in my ear during the night and it wakes me up. I think I have a cold.

column3: There’s a constant throbbing in my left ear and sometimes a ringing. I had a cold two days ago, and the symptoms started the following morning. I also feel tired and have mild fever.

column2: I noticed a sudden rash on my face after a long meeting. The spots itch and flare up whenever I touch them. I’ve never had a rash before. I’m not sure if it’s food-related or something else.
Carrying a backpack on one shoulder left a tight ache under the collarbone toward the armpit. Reaching up and slightly back makes it pinch.
I feel a knot between the shoulder blades that bites when I pull something toward me. Sitting at the computer makes it stiffen again by afternoon.

column3: Every time I open a can of soda, I get a rash on my wrist and hand. The spots are itchy and spread quickly. I don’t eat any soda after that, but they keep appearing. I haven’t had this before.
During a beach day, I forgot to wear a shirt, and the sun burned my back. My skin feels sore and tight.

After three days of a cold, the cough dropped lower. There’s a burning feeling behind the breastbone when I cough and I get short winded climbing stairs. Warm drinks help for an hour, then it comes back.
While vacuuming, the vacuum nozzle caught on my ankle and left a scorch. The skin is red, sore, and there’s a small blister forming.

column2: Sit‑ups left a sore line from the pubic area toward the navel. Sneezing or sitting up quickly gives a pulling pain along the midline.
I’m constantly thirsty and I need to pee frequently. I’ve also been losing weight without trying. My doctor suspects this might be a new health issue.

column3: I feel a pressing pain in my left ear, and my hearing is off. I also notice a slight fluid sensation. It started when I caught a cold last week.
By evening my index knuckle feels puffy and tender to press. Making a tight fist is uncomfortable, especially first thing in the morning.

column2: The hives started on my neck and moved to my chest. They’re not painful but they itch. I had a flu vaccine last month and it might be a trigger.
I can't keep up with my kids; I'm constantly exhausted. I've started to notice my lips look paler and I feel dizzy when I change positions.

column3: I notice the pain starts before sunrise. It’s a pressure behind my eyes that gets worse with light, and I feel nauseated. I’ve never seen a doctor about it.
After lots of screwdriver work, rotating my forearm palm‑up feels weak with a pinpoint ache near the outer elbow. Lifting a kettle that way bothers it.
Pushing up from the floor causes a sharp ache on the outer half of my wrist. Side‑to‑side wiggles of the wrist bring on a quick, painful click.
After throwing a ball, the back‑outer shoulder aches and small, controlled outward rotations feel sharp. Reaching to the side overhead is tight.
I’ve been having a dull ache after a big lunch. The pain gets stronger when I’m in bright light, and I feel like I’m going to vomit.
After a day at the pool, my daughter says her ear feels plugged and tender if we press on it. She had a cold recently. We’re keeping the ear dry and giving an age‑appropriate pain reliever.
I have a sharp pain in my right ear, and I notice a slight fluid sensation. It started after a cold and worsened last night. I have mild fever.
I felt a bit fatigued and had a mild sore throat after a day of heavy work. My ears were also blocked, and I had a clear runny nose. I didn't consider it a serious illness at first.

column2: Every morning I find my coffee mug empty before I even finish it. I also keep feeling very tired even after a full night’s sleep. My weight has dropped about 5 kilograms over the last six weeks.
Reaching for a long step I felt a grab high on the inner back of the thigh. Stretching that leg is tight and I can’t stride out properly.

column3: On the sole, a painful spot feels like stepping on a stone. The surface is rough with tiny dark dots; it hurts when I put pressure on it.

column2: A small sore spot has appeared above the outer ankle bone. Longer walks make it throb and pressing there is very tender.

column3: I touched a heating pad that was too hot. The skin is irritated, slightly swollen, and I see a clear blister on the back of my hand.

column2: I noticed my eyes were blurry after a coffee. I feel exhausted and my feet are tingling. The doctor suggested checking my blood glucose levels.

column3: After weeks of a stubborn cough, there’s a pinpoint sore area along one rib that catches with deep breaths. Laughing or twisting sets it off again.
I slammed my fingertip in a drawer. The tip is throbbing, the nail looks bruised, and even light pressure on the end is painful.
I woke up with a throbbing pain in my left ear, and it feels like a blockage. The pain is worse at night, and I’ve had a low grade fever. I think it might be an ear infection.
During the summer, my throat gets itchy and I start coughing more, especially when the pollen count is high.

column2: Pushing off during a brisk walk makes the big toe joint feel stiff and sore. Bending the toe up is limited and certain shoes aggravate it.

column3: Every morning I wake up with a pounding headache that doesn't go away with caffeine. I thought it was just a migraine. My work schedule is hectic, and I can't remember my last check‑up.
